details.text: Derivative: السُّوأَىٰ is upon the form فُعْلاء (superlative form) and the feminine of أَسْوَأ. It refers to the worst form of evil acts.
Derivative: To be in a state of despair and hopelessness.
Derivative: يَتَفَرَّقُونَ from تَفَرَّقَ which means to be separated, divided and split apart from one another.

Derivative: يُحْبَرُونَ from حَبَرَ – يَحْبُرُ and means to be happy, delighted and filled with joy. حِبْر which is derived from it does not just refer to ink but rather implies beautifying paper with ink. يُحْبَر in the passive form means to be filled with beauty and joy.
Derivative: رَوْضَة is a garden that is full of colour and texture or variety and stimulates the senses.

Derivative: مُحْضَرُونَ means to be presented and brought forth. In simple language مُحْضَر is similar to being called to a boss’s or official’s office for a disciplinary reason, like a summons to a court i.e., to be brought before someone to be reprimanded or judged or to explain yourself.
Derivative: سُبْحَانَ means to declare the perfection.
Derivative: تُمْسُونَ when you are passing through your evenings.
Derivative: تُصْبِحُونَ when you are passing through your mornings.

unlike الحَمْدُ لله and الله أَكْبَر is not a statement of factor but rather implies a call to action. the fat’hah (فَتْحَة َ) implies that there is a verb before it i.e., “declare” the سُبْحَانَ of allah.

Derivative: غُلِبَتِ means to be overcome from غَلَبَwhich means to overcome. It is used when a nation is defeated and loses to another nation in war. غَالِب is the victor than wins and مَغْلوب is the defeated. غُلِبَتِ is the passive form and hence means مَغْلوب (defeated/overcome).
Derivative: مَّوَدَّةً refers to the pure and strong love that manifests itself and is more apparent that other forms of love.

Derivative: تَسْكُنُوا from سَكَنَ which literally means to be still and from that tranquillity and peacefulness emerges. Movement means disturbance whilst سُكون is the absence of movement and disturbance.

Derivative: رَحْمَةً means more than the English word of mercy and implies a special form of love like the love of a mother for a child. It wants to make sure that someone is cared for, is not hurt and does not suffer etc. It encompasses a tenderness and kindness.
- It means “For a people or nation that wants to apply their intellect”.

Derivative: قَانِتُونَ means to be obedient and in submission. It is derived from قَنَتَ – قُنُوتٌ. قُنُوتٌ implies the meaning of being in anticipation of a command so that you have the opportunity to obey i.e., the readiness and enthusiasm to obey.
Derivative: أَهْوَنُ – Means something that is easier or requires less effort.

- It means He (Allah) has the highest description or quality that none other has the like of i.e., every quality or attribute of perfection.
- It can also refer to Allah providing the best and highest examples related to Himself and also that He alone has the ability to provide the best examples for everything.
Derivative: أَدْنَى means the closets or nearest.

Derivative: فِطْرَة means nature, pre-disposition and creation. فَطَرَ means to create something and mould it. The فِطْرَة is that which we have been preprogrammed with and this creation of His has special significance due to its being attributed to Allah when mentioned: فِطْرَتَ اللَّهِ i.e., the Fitrah of Allah.
Derivative: مُنِيبِينَ from أَنابَ – إِنابَة and means to return back to Allah in repentance and with sincerity to submit and obey Him.
Derivative: فَرَّقُوا from فَرَّقَ to break apart into bits and divide up. In respect to the Deen, it means to change, replace and break it apart until it became twisted and distorted.
Derivative: فَرِحُونَ means they were very happy and implies an element of showing off and arrogance.

the first part of the لِيَكْفُرُوا بِمَا آتَيْنَاهُمْ “whereby they deny or show ingratitude for what we have provided them with” is in the third person. the second part then switches to the second person: فَتَمَتَّعُوا فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ “so enjoy yourselves as you will come to know”. it therefore moves from the general address to the direct address, reflecting a threat.
Derivative: يَقْنَطُونَ from قَنِطَ – يَقْنِطُ – قَنُوط which means to be in a state of hopelessness and despair.

- حَقَّهُ “His right”: The verse mentions spending and states that the person should be given his حَقّ (right) and does not merely ask to provide the person with some of your wealth. Rather, the person’s right should be fulfilled and they should be taken care of according to their need.
- حَقَّهُ “His right”: It also means his right in terms of maintain his relation and dealing with him well and with kindness.
- ذَا الْقُرْبَىٰ means those of close relation. This starts with one’s direct family, then those connected by the womb and some scholars have stated that it extends to close friends and neighbours.

Derivative: رِّبًا
- رِّبًا (Riba) from رَبَا – يَرْبُو literally means to increase and grow. In the Quranic context it has a specific meaning which is the increase of money from an investment without any effort, work or risk in the case where the increase is guaranteed e.g. usury from a loan or money placed in a savings account with a return based on an interest rate. In this verse it means: “What you have given (as a loan) with the aim of getting an increase from it or more in return for it from the people”.
Derivative: بِضْعِ refers to a number between three and nine. It is roughly translated as a “few”.

Derivative: يَصَّدَّعُونَ from تَصَدَّعَ which means to be cracked open, broken up and also to be separated from each other which is its intended meaning in this verse i.e., the believers from the disbelievers.

Derivative: يَمْهَدُونَ from مَهَدَ. مَهْدٌ is a cradle and يَمْهَدُونَ means that they are preparing for themselves a place to relax in, a place of ease and comfort i.e., paving the way and preparing for themselves to enter the gardens and ranks of paradise.
Derivative: كِسَفًا separated into pieces or layered; some layers above others.
Derivative: تُثِيرُ to lift and stir (the clouds) like the soil is lifted and stirred when ploughing and cultivating the land.
Derivative: آثَارِ means remnants, traces or clues i.e., that which is left behind to reach and deduce conclusions.

Derivative: مُبْطِلُونَ: مُبْطِل refers to someone that wants to declare others and other things as being false and wrong i.e., the disbelievers say: “You (i.e., the Prophets and believers) just want to declare everything we do and believe in to be false and you just want to cancel our ways and lifestyle”. مُبْطِل can also refer to those who follow falsehood i.e., “You (i.e., the Prophets and believers) are only following and calling for falsehood”.

Derivative: يَطْبَعُ to place a stamp, seal or to imprint something. طَبْعٌ is a seal, print or stamp. طَبْعٌ also means “nature” from the perspective of something being set in a particular way so that it becomes someone’s or something’s nature.
Derivative: يَسْتَخِفَّ – To take lightly, not seriously, think nothing of, belittle, underestimate and look down upon.
Derivative: مُّسَمًّى means declared or specified and also means well-named, in the case that it has been given many names.
Derivative: أَجَلٍ refers to time of expiration or the term of time until it completes and reaches its specified end or time limit.
Derivative: أَثَارُوا from أَثَارَ which means to stir and lift up. Here it refers to tilling, ploughing and cultivating the land.
Derivative: بَيِّنَات is the plural of بَيِّنَة and means clear self-evident truths, proofs, signs or evidence.
Derivative: عَمَرُوا means they built from عَمَرَ which means to give life to the land, to build a home, tall buildings and civilisation.
